videos.title: 2026 BITCOIN PRICE PREDICTION!!!!
videos.text: 2026 Bitcoin price prediction. Hello everyone. It's almost New Year, almost Christmas. But I'm not saying happy Merry Christmas yet and happy New Year. Still have things to discuss, and that is my price target for Bitcoin in 2026. It's been a while. It's been a crazy ride since 2019 in crypto. And a lot of you guys have been following me since then and thank you for your trust and thank you for all the followers and I really truly appreciate I really get a chance to talk to you like this and say these things, but really appreciate every single view, every single comment, every single like. And yeah. When it comes to my target for Bitcoin in 2026, I think first it is worth mentioning that 2025 has been horrible. I got quite disappointed by the Bitcoin price performance, even though in this cycle we've been accumulating Bitcoin together. Hopefully you did with me. The last best price we got Bitcoin at was 16,500 when FTX was collapsing. We bought Bitcoin before, but average entry price 27,000. And I wanted to see this year Bitcoin at least at 200,000, 250,000, and we got to 126 something and just puked back down. Gold performed, you know, way better. So Peter Schiff is winning in short-term race for short-term. But you know, since it's hasn't been great. So my predictions for 2025 were not the best. I still made, what, at the peak 5x, 4 and 1/2 x, and most of my portfolio was Bitcoin, so I'm okay. I've done okay. And I always told you this guys. I bought a lot of [ __ ] coins that did not perform so well, but happily Bitcoin did and a few other coins also did. And trading has done quite well. So it's not as good as hoped, but still was okay. So that's why when it comes to my prediction for Bitcoin for 2026 and the price of Bitcoin in 2026, I want to be extremely careful about how I predict this. And I'm seeing so many predictions of Bitcoin at the million dollars, Bitcoin at 500,000 dollars, Bitcoin at this, Bitcoin at that, but in reality, let's be objective about what determines the possibility of certain price performance on Bitcoin, and what determines where Bitcoin price is going to be. Those who are saying that Bitcoin price is going to be at a million dollars in 2026, what are they actually considering? Like what is it that they're looking at to make that conclusion? You know what I realized? That all these people are full of [ __ ] You know why that is? Because without one single piece of data, you cannot predict [ __ ] And for me, I'm going to tell you what my price prediction is, but what we are waiting for and what is the reason why we did not have a nice price performance in 2025, what we really wanted to see, is due to only just one reason. And that one reason is very simple. Lack of liquidity, quantitative tightening, which ended only on the 1st of December. They've been taking out liquidity out of the market since COVID. So it's for for the last [ __ ] 3 years. They're fighting inflation. What did we expect from meme coins when people are poor, extra like extreme levels of the record levels of credit card debt, extreme low levels of personal savings, no money printing, companies are starving, just banking system is starting to struggle. There is a repo crisis at our [ __ ] hands for God's sake. And we are expecting meme coins to pump? We're expecting for Bitcoin to go to 200,000? So it's all nonsense. Therefore, it's easy to conclude that all that we really care about in 2026 is to seek quantitative easing. And this is what I've been talking about in so many of my past videos, and this is the only thing that can hit save us. This is the only thing that when the Fed injects liquidity and buys long-term bonds and other assets into its balance sheet, debt becomes cheaper, there is more money in the system, people maybe get some more money, maybe some stimulus checks, God knows what. And then there's going to be a lot more money in old coins and meme coins because people are going to have extra money to [ __ ] gamble. So it's all just interconnected. Therefore, for us to really make any kind of Bitcoin price prediction for 2026, we need to estimate and understand how much of that money printing there is going to be. Is it going to be just short-term T-bills like right now, like this repo plugging the holes in the repo thing, or before May when Jerome Powell leaves his seat as the chair of Federal Reserve and Trump appoints somebody friendly to him who's going to be inclined to print money, and they going to really turn on the money printer and print trillions of dollars of fresh new capital. If that's going to happen, it's a different [ __ ] story, but do we know which one of these two is going to happen? Is going to be mild QE, crazy QE, or something in the middle? Those who say that they know are full of [ __ ] because they don't. Therefore, in my personal humble opinion, last time we had a repo crisis, which was in 2019, it was before COVID happened, we had Bitcoin go from 3K to 13K. And then COVID happened, we actually went into full on QE mode, Bitcoin fell down to 4K again, we went to 69K. So the power of real full on QE is different from a repo crisis plugging the holes of short-term I don't know, T-bills in repo facility. So that being said, I think the 2026 is going to be a good year. Or at least I want to believe it. But these goddamn flies, goddammit. But the question here is how good is it going to be? And that I cannot answer. My least favorable target for Bitcoin where I want to really sell or start considering selling my Bitcoin is going to be 150,000 dollars a Bitcoin. And that is what I had in my mind even this year. If it would have hit 130 to 150, this range, I would consider taking profits there and just [ __ ] retiring. But 2026, if we're going to see some improvements in terms of liquidity and we're going to see some promise from the government to do crazy stimulus, I would not be surprised to see Bitcoin at our original target of 200, 250, or maybe even 300,000 dollars. Because if they print like crazy, especially in response to something bad happening and system completely breaking, I mean, this would be the real bull market. This would be the [ __ ] Valhalla. So all we got to hope for is not a small QE, but actual powerful one just like after COVID. Because system needs money, and it needs a lot of it for us to see the pumps that we want to see. So let's see, 250 to 300 in 2026. Let's go.

videos.text: Yo, welcome everyone. Bitcoin is now getting a rejection back down, but this was pretty much expected. Let me now show you what move will most people probably miss. videos.title: Raoul Pal :"My NEW Prediction For Ethereum & Bitcoin In 2026" (Prepare Now)
videos.text: Four years. That's how long Raoul Pal says the world is left before it enters the most uncertain economic stretch most of us will ever live through. And in a recent conversation, he laid out exactly why he thinks there's only one asset built to carry you through to the other side. This isn't even a fresh prediction. It's the same call he's been making for years, except now the clock has moved from six years out to four. And what's changed his own thinking recently isn't just the debasement of currency he's talked about for over a decade. It's what's happening on top of it, autonomous agents transacting and entire markets moving on chain, all stacking on the same trend at the same time. So, today we're breaking down exactly what he said, the reasoning behind the numbers, and the framework he uses to decide when to buy and why he refuses to ever sell. Let's get into it. >> I've obviously known this for a long time, but I'm just realizing now with the agents and the fun with all of the things we've talked about, the endless debasement of currency, plus the agents, plus um everything going on chain, it's like, why would you sell this? >> This is a massive shift in mindset that I realized, I think, gets much easier when you start to generate quite a lot of a lot of income, right? >> Yeah. >> Because otherwise you you might have a job which is the majority of the people, and you make money, but your dreams and hope are in crypto, and you but you're like, I want to trade this thing because maybe my income every month is good, but is not enabling me to to have an exceptional life, right? And I will need so much time. But when you start to make more income, this becomes so much easier. Yeah, it is it is. And I know people you know, it's hard for everybody. It's hard. Um but really if you if you logically think about it, we know where the world is going. We kind of know where the market cap is going to be over time cuz it's following Metcalfe's law and that you can just draw the log regression channel. So, what why would you ever sell it? >> Yeah, never sell and buy every month. Each I mean at some point you get back to like the very basics after so many years, which is just just just never sell and just buy every month. >> So, that it's the left curve and right curve is here, right? And as I said, I mean this is humanity's pension plan. This is what this is now cuz we get to invest in the infrastructure rails of which all the agentic economy will run. We get to do that. It's a pension plan. It'll bail you out of everything that's happening. You just have to do it, which is why, you know, I first said the economic singularity is coming and you got at the time 6 years. And now it's what? 4 years away. You've got 4 years to just own as much of this [ __ ] as possible before you go into the biggest moments of uncertainty, but I think blockchain survived through the other side. Corporations, I've no idea what's going to happen over time. But blockchains? Oh, yeah. >> Pal's starting point is simple. Currency debasement has been happening for a long time and he's been vocal about it for years. What's shifted is that he now sees two more forces layering on top of that same trend. Autonomous agents are beginning to transact on their own and increasing amounts of economic activity are settling on chain. Put those three things together and his question becomes almost rhetorical. Why would anyone sell an asset positioned at the center of all three? He frames Bitcoin's trajectory through Metcalfe's law, the idea that a network's value grows with the square of its users. Draw that curve out and plot it against a log regression channel and you get a rough sense of where the market is heading over time, not week to week, but across cycles. That's also where his never sell, buy every month philosophy comes from. It's less a trading strategy and more a mechanical habit, removing emotion from the equation entirely. He goes further, calling Bitcoin humanity's pension plan, not because it replaces a 401k, but because it gives ordinary people a way to own a piece of the I infrastructure that the I gigantic economy will eventually run on. That's the same logic behind his economic singularity thesis. The idea that debasement, automation, and on-chain settlement converge into a single moment of economic disruption. He first flagged that moment as 6 years away. Today, he puts it at four. Whatever happens to individual corporations along the way, his view is that the underlying blockchain rails make it through intact. If you found this breakdown useful so far, consider subscribing. It helps this channel keep bringing you these conversations in full. >> So, I I have done work cuz I've built an been building an indicator for Real Vision to help people with this. >> Mhm. >> And what I basically said is if it's two standard deviations oversold on that log regression channel between one and two, you buy. Um it compounds at a stupid rate. Now, if you if you sell, how much do you sell at the top of the cycle? Here's the problem. Or how much do you reinvest back in the bottom? That's the hard part. If you Let's say you get one two opportunities to to add every 4 years. Maybe one in the middle cycle, one at the end of the cycle. You got two opportunities. You should save up money between each one of those. Do nothing, do nothing, do nothing. You get that like we've just had, you buy. You go away, you keep saving money, you do that again. Okay, that's doable for most people. Just keep adding to your bet as much as you can. That obviously outperforms everything because you never sell. If you sell at two standard deviations, how much do you sell? Or one standard deviation overbought? Two gives you the least signals, but it's a better quality signal. And this is where the problem is. If you take 25% off, yes, you've got lifestyle chips, but you then you've got less money in the casino. If you take 50% off and you say, "Well, I'm going to add that back at the bottom, good luck doing that. >> Naturally, a bold thesis needs a system behind it and Pal has one. He's built an indicator specifically designed to flag the moments worth acting on. His rule is straightforward. When Bitcoin trades between one and two standard deviations below its long-term log regression channel, that's the signal to buy. Historically, entries at that level have compounded at a rate few other strategies come close to matching. The harder question, in his view, isn't when to buy. It's what to do at the top. His answer is to treat the whole cycle as a savings discipline rather than a trading strategy. Most people only get one or two genuine buying windows across a 4-year cycle, maybe one mid-cycle and one near the end. In between, the job is simple. Save, do nothing, and wait for the next window to open. Then, repeat. He's candid about why selling is the part that trips people up. Take a smaller slice off the top for lifestyle spending and you're left with less capital compounding going forward. Take a much larger slice off with the intention of buying back in at the bottom and you're now depending on perfect timing twice in a row, once to sell and once to re-enter. It's a plan that sounds reasonable on a whiteboard and rarely survives contact with an actual market cycle. >> Cuz people can't do it. We're seeing it now. >> Yeah, it's too difficult. >> People can't do it. They're doing it now and they're chasing all the way up. And you you will tend to [ __ ] it up. So, on paper, you could be an absolute amazing compound performer if you could buy the bottoms and sell the tops. I've been in the industry for 35 years and I don't know anybody who can do it. >> [snorts] >> I saw you recently on this traders podcast. And I I mean, I I have a bunch of friends who are traders and I I mean, we all know all this stuff. But like, it was kind of funny because you were saying that on a traders podcast when this guy is literally selling trading to all his audience. And the truth is the truth, right? >> there are Look at how many traders there are and look at how many of them actually make a lot of money consistently over time from trading. >> So, people would say Paul Tudor Jones, Louis Bacon, Stan [clears throat] Druckenmiller. Yeah, they also made a lot of money out of asset management fees. >> Um That's where they made a huge amount of money after they got big. But, yes, some of those guys Louis, Paul, and Stan are all like five standard deviations talent. They just don't exist. Most people don't make money. Why is it that every brokerage firm, their most profitable customers the people who make the most P&L are dead ones? I mean, I've seen more and more of these crypto traders who are >> not And there's not many who are not like loud mouths and who used to make money trading crypto in 2019, 2020, 2021 saying I lost my edge now and the only money I made I mean, I made a lot of money, but I lost it all, right? The only And I still made a lot of money, but it's just because I was buying I was holding Bitcoin. And I was doing nothing with it. And all the money I made actually comes from that. >> Yes. >> This is where Pal's own background comes in. With three and a half decades inside the finance industry, he's watched enough market cycles to know how rare consistent trading success actually is. Even naming the legends, Paul Tudor Jones, Louis Bacon, and Stanley Druckenmiller comes with a caveat. A meaningful share of their fortunes came later from asset management fees once their funds scaled, not purely from trade by trade returns. And the traders who did generate outsized returns purely from calling markets right are, in his words, five standard deviations of talent. Statistically, that kind of outlier barely exists. He points to something brokerage firms have quietly known for years. Their most profitable accounts, the ones with the best long-term returns, tend to belong to clients who simply forgot they had the account. No trading, no emotional excess, just time in the market. That pattern shows up inside crypto, too. Traders who built real followings buying and selling Bitcoin during 2019 through 2021 have increasingly admitted that whatever edge they once had has faded. And when they trace back where their actual long-term gains came from, it wasn't the trading. It was the Bitcoin they bought early and simply held. >> And look at the people who made the most money out of crypto are the people who don't trade it. >> You know, if you use the example of people like Wences Casares or even Dan Morehead, all of these guys have made a fortune by doing precisely [ __ ] all. >> And yet everybody wants to be the next trader who scalps it and it'll It's like I I I don't get it. I just don't get it. >> [laughter] >> And then it's all about emotion and it's like angry when it goes down and like overjoys when it goes up and it's like it's so it's so such a waste of energy. >> You were saying doing [ __ ] all. >> Dan Tapiero would say >> you're being patient. Which is very different. >> It is. >> It's actually like you did the thing, you bought the thing and you're being It's not that you need to reframe your mindset, which is uh And I think it's Paul Tudor Jones who said I want to make money in a trade or long-term trade by staying as long as possible, right? Which is the same thing. >> Yes. And so go back to this universal code framework. Output of intelligence, the intelligence is the trade working out, compounding its capital, all of that. The units of energy. >> Just think of that. Think of the units of energy use of buying some Bitcoin and doing nothing. That is the most efficient trade you'll ever do in your life. But what we do is the in- inefficient, which is use vast amounts of our energy, mental energy, emotional energy, because we want to trade it. So you what you're creating is a system that is by definition less optimal. >> Pahl's closing point reframes what doing nothing actually means. He points to early Bitcoin holders like Wences Casares and Dan Morehead, people who who serious wealth in the space specifically by not trading it. Meanwhile, an entire industry keeps chasing the identity of the trader who scalps every swing, riding the anger of a red day and the euphoria of a green one. To him, that emotional cycle is simply wasted energy with nothing to show for it on a long enough timeline. He reaches for a physics-style framework to make the point land. Every action, financial or otherwise, has an energy cost. Buying Bitcoin and holding it requires almost none. Trading it, watching it, and reacting to every move burn enormous amounts of mental and emotional energy for a strategy that historically underperforms simply sitting still. In his framing, that makes constant trading a less efficient system by definition, regardless of anyone's individual skill. It's a different way of stating the same conclusion Paul Tudor Jones once reached from a very different angle, that some of the best returns come not from being right the most often, but from staying in a position the longest. So, where does that leave things? Pal's argument opened with a countdown to 4 years until the economic singularity he's been forecasting for the better part of a decade arrives. Everything he laid out afterwards builds towards that same point from a different direction. The macro case rests on debasement, automation, and on-chain settlement converging at once. The strategy case rests on a mechanical buy signal paired with the discipline to do nothing in between. The behavioral case rests on decades of data showing that even elite operators rarely beat simply holding. And the closing case reframes patience itself as the more efficient trade, not the passive one. None of that guarantees the next 4 years play out exactly as he expects. Markets have a habit of humbling anyone who claims certainty, but the through line across everything he said is consistent, and it's the same idea he's been repeating in different forms for years. Time in the asset has done more work than timing the asset ever has. Whether that holds for the next cycle the way it held for the last one is the question every holder eventually has to answer for themselves. See you in the next one.

videos.title: BUY BITCOIN AT: $XX,XXX! Bitcoin Price Prediction 2026
videos.text: Hey guys, what's going on? Jeff here and in today's video we are going to be talking about why I do not believe that the Bitcoin bear market is over just yet. We have so many people crying from the rooftops, "Bear market's over. 60k was the bottom. Time to start rallying." And I am here to tell you that the vast majority of people that are telling you that are probably telling you that because they took out a bunch of debt to buy Bitcoin up at the top. These are the kind of people that are probably telling you that because they are extremely emotionally invested in Bitcoin going up right now because they're holding some heavy bags and they cannot stand the thought of Bitcoin going through a bear market for the rest of 2026. So in today's video, as always, I'm going to show you where I believe that Bitcoin is going not based on my opinion, not based on my emotions, but based on facts, evidence, charts, and analysis, and then most importantly, I'm going to explain to you exactly how you should play the 2026 bear market so that you can set yourself up for the rest of this decade because this year this year, man, is going to bring some incredible investment opportunities. So without much further ado, let's go ahead and dive right on into it. Today's show is brought to you in part by NordVPN, more on that later. Bitcoin, as you can see, is currently trading at $67,000 and the bounce off of 60,000 has been nothing short of weak. That is the only word for it. It has been weak. Bitcoin has only managed to rally back up to, on the log chart, the .382 Fibonacci level and on the regular chart, which will become important in just a second, as you can see, we haven't even gotten there. Resistance sits here at $75,000 and then there's even stronger resistance up here at $80,000. Now, it's very interesting because our overall thesis of this market is that we are more or less tracking with the Elliott Wave 1 2 3 4 5 pattern that is most likely going to have us bottoming out somewhere in the high 40s to possibly the low to mid 50s at some point in the next couple of uh in the next couple of quarters. So probably sometime around the end of summer. This is because Bitcoin thus far this cycle has been tracking almost perfectly with the previous bull market and bear market. Our our most recent bull market was very similar to our most recent bear market and our bear market that we're in right now is very similar to our previous bull market. The fractal that you could draw here is virtually identical when you kind of overlay it. You can see you have the two tops, one of them is slightly higher than the one before, but because of the weakness, we ended up going through the bear market right on time to the very day that the four-year having cycle would predict that it would take place. And if we do indeed follow through on the exact same structure that we saw in the last cycle, what we probably just saw was that we are either right here or we are right here. And nonetheless, the bounce and recovery is probably not going to send us anywhere into the one zone. As you can see in our last bear market, the one wave, the first wave to the downside, bottomed out at approximately $34,000. Bitcoin would not see $34,000 again, not one time, not for one second until the bull market resumed. And when we did actually break through $34,000, all hell broke loose. Anywhere in this zone was an incredible buying zone. And in our current market, that's basically the same thing as saying anywhere beneath $80,000 is an incredible entry. Now, I want to back up. Some people are going to hear what I say on this channel and think, "Oh my gosh, Jeff's a permabear. Bitcoin's going to zero. Bitcoin's just going to be in a bear market for four years." No, I don't think that. I think that Bitcoin is going through a traditional four-year having cycle bear market and I think it's about two-thirds the size of previous of the previous bear market, which means I believe it'll probably be about 60 or so percent deep instead of about 80% deep, about 78% deep, which is what we saw last cycle. And so that does not mean that right now is the time that you should be selling. If anything, this is a buy zone. But what I want you to understand is that this is not yet our strong buy zone. I think that you're going to get better entries and right now would be a very dangerous time to try and just go absolutely crazy with a bunch of high leverage longs, which is why we're dropping. People are getting wiped out. So if we are in the exact same zone in this market as we were in this market, then what does that mean? Well, what that means is that it is highly unlikely going to break above $80,000 and if it does, it's going to be very brief. Why would we break above $80,000 in the first place? One reason that we might break, although briefly, above $80,000 is because you have a CME futures gap up here. I personally am of the opinion and certainly of the hope that this CME futures gap does not get filled because if Bitcoin's bear market does drop us back down towards 48 to $53,000, this CME futures gap is going to be hanging over our heads as this bull just gold gorgeous pie in the sky target. And so it's going to make entries down in the $50,000 range very attractive because Bitcoin basically always fills CME futures gap, which basically means that you're looking at like a baked-in 60% return. And that's on top of the fact that people are already just considering, "Oh, well if we just go back to all-time high, then Bitcoin's going to rally 90%." And that is what is going to make investment in Bitcoin more and more attractive as you start to go down towards the 60k region. You see, you have to be very careful in Bitcoin because there are narratives everywhere. There's narratives of, "Oh my goodness, quantum killed Bitcoin."
